subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Indian official (MoSPI, RBI) + Indian independent press (The Hindu, Indian Express, Scroll.in, The Wire, Down to Earth).
Politics: a Modi/BJP-led NDA third term that lost its outright single-party majority in 2024 - stable but coalition-dependent - against a multi-year democratic-backsliding trend.

Standout drags: democratic backsliding, very low press freedom, severe climate/air-quality exposure, and weak public healthcare. Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone, since Indian official and elite-press output is largely in English.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: Strong nominal growth, a credible central bank, and cleaned-up banks, against a high public-debt load and an acute near-term external-tariff shock.

fiscal_state.notes: High general-government debt ~80% GDP on a slow downward glide; central deficit ~4.4% on a credible consolidation path; strong nominal growth flatters the ratios. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The RBI is a credible inflation-targeter (repo ~5.25%, neutral stance), with some government-pressure nuance on rate-cut timing and governor turnover. (RBI MPC)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The rupee is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Energy-import-price shock from the 2026 Hormuz closure (a large share of India's crude and LNG transits the strait) lifts near-term inflation. (Reuters; BBC)

banking_stability.notes: Post-NPA cleanup complete: gross NPAs ~2.1% (a multi-decade low), strong capital and profitability; emerging unsecured-retail risk. (RBI FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Record ~$430bn exports and resilient IT/services, plus a 'Make in India' manufacturing push, against an acute near-term US-tariff shock; no resource curse. (IMF; commerce data)

institutional.notes: Independent courts and credible electoral machinery, undercut by backlogs, selective enforcement, and a very low press-freedom standing.

rule_of_law.notes: Independent courts but huge case backlogs and selective UAPA/FCRA enforcement; a multi-year WJP decline. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: A new 2022-23 base-year GDP series is a real improvement, but real-growth discrepancies and informal-sector coverage gaps persist; IMF data-quality reservations. (IMF; MoSPI)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable technocratic pockets (the RBI, the Election Commission machinery) against broad bureaucratic slowness. (observed)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 151/180 ('very serious'): ownership concentration, political interference, and reporter-safety problems. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A genuine demographic dividend, offset by democratic backsliding, restricted civil liberties, and communal tensions.

political_stability.notes: A Modi/BJP-led NDA third term that lost its outright single-party majority in 2024 - stable but coalition-dependent; V-Dem classifies it an electoral autocracy. (Election results; V-Dem 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: Hindu-nationalist majoritarianism, CAA/NRC, minority/Muslim pressure, frequent internet shutdowns, and criminalisation of dissent. (Freedom House; CIVICUS)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited long-term routes for non-Indians; OCI status for the diaspora only; not a relocation-friendly regime. (statute)

social_cohesion.notes: Communal and religious tensions, caste cleavages, and sharp regional fault lines. (observed)

demographics.notes: The world's most populous country with a median age ~29 and a large working-age share - a genuine dividend; sub-replacement fertility (~1.9) means the window narrows. (UNFPA 2025)

geopolitical.notes: Pragmatic strategic autonomy, against live tensions with Pakistan and along the China border.

alliance_reliability.notes: Multi-aligned strategic autonomy - Quad plus enduring Russia ties - pragmatic, with few binding commitments and current US trade friction. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: A May 2025 flare-up with Pakistan (Operation Sindoor, then a ceasefire) and an unresolved China LAC standoff - mostly disengaged but sensitive. (Stimson; Carnegie)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Managed capital controls; no major sanctions regime against it. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: Fast-improving infrastructure, against severe climate/air-quality exposure, weak public healthcare, and low catastrophe-insurance cover.

climate.notes: Severe heatwaves/wet-bulb in the north plains, monsoon variability, water stress, coastal flooding; large but uneven adaptive capacity. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Low public health spend (~1.9% GDP) and high out-of-pocket costs, with a strong private/medical-tourism tier; very uneven. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving fast (digital public infrastructure/UPI, highways) but congested and uneven. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate with wide regional variation; serious women's-safety concerns and low conviction rates. (NCRB)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low catastrophe-insurance penetration (~half the global average) against high hazard exposure. (IRDAI; Swiss Re)

summary: A fast-growing economy with a genuine demographic dividend - a young, large workforce - plus a credible central bank, cleaned-up banks, and inflation within target. The drags are a high public-debt load, democratic backsliding (an electoral-autocracy classification, internet shutdowns, criminalised dissent), a very low press-freedom standing, severe climate and air-quality exposure, and weak public healthcare. Strategic autonomy keeps it non-aligned amid live tensions with Pakistan and along the China border.
